name: m16-gdn-dvtiling-economics-cb
purpose: Close the T1 'owed' item: measure the GDN chain dv-tiling's effect on MTP C_B and acceptance on a REAL prompt (the degenerate 1-token smoke cannot assess acceptance). Clean same-prompt A/B of strict (shuffle baseline, HEAD~1) vs relaxed (dv-tiled, HEAD).
workload: quicksort_prefix (90 tok, fixtures/dflash/stable_prompts.jsonl), decode-tokens=32, chain-attn-mode=batched, proposal_impl=persistent_device, B=3, gate off
command: scripts/mtp_verifier_economics.py --prompt-tokens-file <quicksort 90 ids> --decode-tokens 32 --candidate-budgets 3 --runs 3 --chain-attn-mode batched --backend hip_gfx1100 --hip-arch gfx1100 --proposal-impl persistent_device
finding: C_B UNCHANGED within run-to-run noise: 4.81 +/- 0.14 (strict) vs 4.80 +/- 0.28 (relaxed); delta -0.016 is ~30x smaller than the std. Acceptance is byte-IDENTICAL (0.4615, std 0, same accepted_lengths every run) and all_exact_ar_match=true for ALL 6 runs -- the T1 relaxation flips exact_ar_match ONLY on the degenerate 1-token smoke prompt, never on the real prompt. The rocprof-confirmed -0.56 ms/pass GDN kernel saving is below the economics noise floor (cycle-wall std ~0.6-1.2 ms) because the verify cycle is dispatch/host-bound (~19.4 ms dispatch floor), not kernel-bound.
conclusions: T1 (relaxed, KL-gated) is SAFE on real prompts: zero acceptance/exactness change vs strict.
Kernel-time relaxation alone does NOT move C_B at B=3 -- reinforces docs/MEGAKERNEL.md S9.2: C_B is dispatch-floor-bound. dv-tiling is banked kernel-time headroom.
T1 'owed' item (re-baseline acceptance on real prompts) is CLOSED: acceptance preserved.
related: docs/RELAXED.md S0.1 (first landed relaxed profile, full characterization)
